channel.title: Posts on DELs personal site
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on DELs personal site

item.title: Modelling personal risk of COVID19 part 1: Why large groups are a really bad idea right now
item.description: Introduction Danger of groups The birthday problem or how infrequent events can be suprsingly common. If the incidence of something is low, then you could correctly guess that it didn’t happen and be right most of the time. For example, if you were to guess that a randomly selected male does not have breast cancer, you would be right 99.9973% of the time. However, just because something is rare, that doesn’t preclude it from happening.

item.title: Why COVID-19 is still serious even if you ignore mortality
item.description: Introduction The effects from COVID-19 can be permanent and severe Short-term consequences of infection can have long-term impacts Early suggestions of chronic, long term consequences of infections The effects from COVID-19 can also be mild Some people never realize they have been infected Asymptomatic is not the same as unaffected Conclusion Introduction There has been quite a lot of focus on using the infection fatality rate (IFR)1 of COVID-19 to quantify the disease severity.

item.title: Introduction to COVID-19 posts
item.description: Perspective I want to be done this pandemic and to just get on with things. Unfortunately, SARS-COV-2 has other ideas. The reality is we’re just going to have to live it with for the time being. What this means of course varies from country to country. I’m an American who lives in Pennsylvania with family and friends in various locations scattered throughout the U.S. So everything I write will necessarily be colored by that perspective.

item.title: Modelling personal risk of COVID19 part 1: Why large groups are a really bad idea right now
item.description: Introduction I—like everyone else in the world—wants to be done this pandemic and to just get on with things. Unfortunately, COVID19 has other ideas. The reality is we’re just going to have to live it with for the time being. This of course varies from country to country. I’m an American who lives in Pennsylvania with family and friends in various locations scattered throughout the U.S. So everything I write will necessarily be colored by that perspective.
